# sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com -- Full Content

This file contains the full text content of all pages on sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com.
Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org).


---

## Credit Rebuilding After SBA Bankruptcy - Starting Over
Source: https://sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com/credit-rebuilding-sba.html

Credit Rebuilding After SBA Bankruptcy

Starting Over

Home » Credit Rebuilding After SBA Bankruptcy

Immediate Steps

Check credit reports, dispute inaccuracies, get a secured credit card, keep non-discharged obligations current. Rebuilding starts the day of discharge.

Recovery Timeline

Improvement within 6-12 months. Unsecured credit in 12-24 months. Auto loans in 12-18 months. FHA mortgage in 2 years after Ch.7 or 1 year into Ch.13.

Starting a New Business

Bankruptcy does not prevent new businesses. Form new entities, get EIN, open accounts. SBA loans unavailable initially but alternative financing can fill the gap. Many successful entrepreneurs have bankruptcy in their past.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I get another SBA loan?

Not immediately. Generally need 2-3 years of positive credit history and 680+ score. Prior loans must be resolved (discharge counts).

Will vendors work with me?

Most will. Business relationships are based on current behavior. Offer cash or prepay initially to build trust.

How do I explain the bankruptcy?

Be direct. Explain circumstances and what changed. Investors care more about your plan and integrity than credit history.

Check your bankruptcy discharge eligibility with our free screening tool.
Free Discharge Screener

About This Data: Content based on federal bankruptcy law (Title 11, U.S. Code) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. This is educational content, not legal advice.

## EIDL and SBA Loan Crossover - Multiple SBA Loans in Bankruptcy
Source: https://sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com/eidl-crossover.html

EIDL and SBA Loan Crossover

Multiple SBA Loans in Bankruptcy

Home » EIDL and SBA Loan Crossover

Multiple Loans in One Case

If you have both an EIDL and a 7(a) or 504 loan, all can be addressed in a single filing. Each is listed as a separate claim. Discharge covers all personal guarantees.

Coordinating Treatment

Each loan's lien is analyzed separately. The SBA may hold EIDL directly while 7(a) involves a private lender. List each creditor separately.

Strategic Considerations

Subchapter V handles all business and personal debts in one case. If the business is closed, Chapter 7 eliminates all guarantees in ~4 months.

Frequently Asked Questions

Does multiple SBA debt affect eligibility?

No limit on amount to discharge. For Ch.13, debts must be under $2.75M. For Subchapter V, under $7.5M.

Can the SBA offset my refund while I'm in bankruptcy?

No. The automatic stay prevents all offsets for all debts listed in the petition.

What about PPP loans?

Forgiven PPP is not a debt. Unforgiven PPP converted to SBA loans is treated the same as any other SBA loan.

Check your bankruptcy discharge eligibility with our free screening tool.
Free Discharge Screener

## SBA Bankruptcy - Small Business Loan Relief [2026]
Source: https://sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com/

Skip to main content

SBA Bankruptcy

Discharge SBA Loans and Personal Guarantees

SBA Loans Are Dischargeable

Despite what many business owners believe, SBA loans - including 7(a), 504, EIDL, and microloans - are fully dischargeable in bankruptcy. There is no special exception for government-backed business loans. Your personal guarantee liability is eliminated upon discharge, and the SBA cannot pursue you personally afterward.

The key question is not whether the debt can be discharged, but which chapter of bankruptcy best fits your situation: Chapter 7 for a clean break, Chapter 13 for individual repayment, or Subchapter V Chapter 11 for business reorganization.

The Personal Guarantee Problem

SBA loans require personal guarantees from business owners with 20% or more ownership. When the business fails, the personal guarantee makes you individually liable for the full loan balance - potentially hundreds of thousands of dollars. The SBA can pursue your personal assets, garnish wages, intercept tax refunds, and file lawsuits.

Bankruptcy eliminates this personal guarantee obligation. In Chapter 7, it is discharged completely in about 4 months. In Chapter 13 or Subchapter V, you pay what you can afford over 3-5 years. The discharge injunction permanently bars the SBA from collecting on your guarantee.

Your Options by SBA Loan Type

SBA 7(a) Loans: The most common SBA loan. Personal guarantee is dischargeable. Collateral liens survive but personal liability is eliminated.

SBA 504 Loans: Real estate and equipment loans with two lien holders (bank + CDC). Can surrender property and discharge the deficiency, or restructure through Subchapter V.

EIDL Loans: COVID-era disaster loans. Fully dischargeable with no special restrictions. See our companion site for detailed EIDL bankruptcy guidance.

SBA Microloans: Loans up to $50,000 through nonprofit intermediaries. May not require personal guarantees. Fully dischargeable.

Explore All Topics

SBA 7(a) Loans in Bankruptcy - Discharge and options

SBA 504 Loans in Bankruptcy - Real estate and equipment

Discharging Personal Guarantees - Eliminating liability

SBA Microloan Default - Options and bankruptcy

EIDL and SBA Loan Crossover - Multiple loans in one case

Subchapter V for SBA Borrowers - Small business reorganization

SBA Collections Timeline - What to expect after default

Alternatives to Bankruptcy - OIC, workout, deed in lieu

Credit Rebuilding After SBA Bankruptcy - Starting over

Frequently Asked Questions

Can SBA loans be discharged in bankruptcy?

Yes. SBA loans - including 7(a), 504, EIDL, and microloans - are fully dischargeable. Unlike student loans, there is no special exception. Personal guarantee liability is eliminated upon discharge.

What happens to my personal guarantee in bankruptcy?

Your personal guarantee obligation is discharged. In Chapter 7, immediately. In Chapter 13 or Subchapter V, you pay what you can afford over 3-5 years and the remainder is discharged. The SBA cannot pursue you personally after discharge.

Can I keep my business and file bankruptcy?

Yes. Subchapter V of Chapter 11 allows you to restructure SBA debt while continuing operations. You remain in control. Most small businesses with SBA debt qualify for Subchapter V if total debts are under $7.5 million.

Check your bankruptcy discharge eligibility with our free screening tool.

Free Discharge Screener

and SBA Standard Operating Procedures. District-level statistics from the

## Discharging SBA Personal Guarantees - Eliminating Liability
Source: https://sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com/personal-guarantee-discharge.html

Discharging SBA Personal Guarantees

Eliminating Liability

Home » Discharging SBA Personal Guarantees

The Problem

SBA loans require personal guarantees from 20%+ owners. When the business fails, you are personally liable for the full balance - $50,000 to $5 million.

Chapter 7 Discharge

Eliminates personal guarantee completely. File individually, list the guarantee as a debt, receive discharge. The SBA is permanently barred from collecting.

Impact on Business Entity

Your personal bankruptcy only discharges your liability. The LLC/corporation still owes. If the entity has assets, the SBA may pursue them. Some file both simultaneously.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can my spouse be liable?

Only if they also signed the guarantee. SBA requires guarantees from owners, not non-owner spouses.

What if I guaranteed multiple loans?

All are dischargeable in a single filing. No limit on number or amount.

Negotiate or file?

The SBA has an offer in compromise program. Bankruptcy eliminates the entire guarantee with no payment on the unsecured portion.

Check your bankruptcy discharge eligibility with our free screening tool.
Free Discharge Screener

About This Data: Content based on federal bankruptcy law (Title 11, U.S. Code) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. This is educational content, not legal advice.

## SBA 504 Loans in Bankruptcy - Real Estate and Equipment
Source: https://sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com/sba-504-loans.html

SBA 504 Loans in Bankruptcy

Real Estate and Equipment

Home » SBA 504 Loans in Bankruptcy

504 Loan Structure

Two components: conventional bank loan (50%) and SBA-backed CDC debenture (40%). Borrower provides 10% equity. In bankruptcy, you may have two creditors with different lien positions.

Real Estate Collateral

Bank holds first lien, CDC holds second. Liens survive discharge. To keep the property, continue paying or include in a plan. If you surrender, the deficiency is dischargeable.

Strategies

Surrender and discharge deficiency in Ch.7, restructure through Subchapter V, negotiate a short sale, or deed in lieu of foreclosure.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I keep my building?

Yes, through Chapter 13 or Subchapter V. Continue regular payments and cure arrears through the plan.

What if the property is underwater?

Surrender and discharge the deficiency in Ch.7. Or in Subchapter V, modify the loans.

Is the CDC loan treated differently?

Both are secured claims. The bank has priority. If property value does not cover both, the CDC's unsecured deficiency is general unsecured.

Check your bankruptcy discharge eligibility with our free screening tool.
Free Discharge Screener

About This Data: Content based on federal bankruptcy law (Title 11, U.S. Code) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. This is educational content, not legal advice.

## SBA 7(a) Loans in Bankruptcy - Discharge and Options
Source: https://sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com/sba-7a-loans.html

SBA 7(a) Loans in Bankruptcy

Discharge and Options

Home » SBA 7(a) Loans in Bankruptcy

7(a) Loan Basics

The SBA 7(a) program provides loans up to $5 million. The SBA guarantees 75-85%. In bankruptcy, the loan is treated as a regular secured or unsecured claim. The SBA guarantee does not affect your discharge rights.

Personal Guarantee

7(a) loans require personal guarantees from anyone owning 20%+. In bankruptcy, the guarantee is dischargeable. Chapter 7 eliminates it completely. Chapter 13 and Subchapter V address it through a plan.

Collateral and Liens

Loans over $25,000 require collateral. In bankruptcy, the lien survives discharge but personal liability is eliminated. The difference between collateral value and loan amount is unsecured and dischargeable.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I discharge a 7(a) loan in Chapter 7?

Yes. Your personal liability is fully dischargeable. The SBA guarantee does not create a non-dischargeability exception.

What happens to the SBA guarantee?

The lender claims the guarantee, SBA pays, then SBA becomes the creditor. The SBA can file a claim in your case but personal liability is still dischargeable.

Should I negotiate or file bankruptcy?

Try negotiating first. If debt is too large, bankruptcy provides a definitive solution.

Check your bankruptcy discharge eligibility with our free screening tool.
Free Discharge Screener

About This Data: Content based on federal bankruptcy law (Title 11, U.S. Code) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. This is educational content, not legal advice.

## Alternatives to Bankruptcy - For SBA Borrowers
Source: https://sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com/sba-alternatives.html

Alternatives to Bankruptcy

For SBA Borrowers

Home » Alternatives to Bankruptcy

Offer in Compromise

SBA Form 770. Typical settlements 10-50%. SBA considers income, assets, borrowing ability, and collection cost.

Workout with the Lender

Before the guarantee triggers: modification, forbearance, or settlement. Lenders prefer workouts because triggering the guarantee affects their metrics.

Deed in Lieu

For collateralized loans: surrender property for a release of the deficiency. Get the release in writing.

Assignment for Benefit of Creditors

State-law business closure alternative. Does NOT discharge personal guarantees.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which alternative is best?

Can afford settlement: OIC. Business viable: workout. Property underwater: deed in lieu. Business closing: ABC + personal bankruptcy.

Can I combine alternatives?

Yes. Deed in lieu for collateral, OIC for deficiency, and bankruptcy as backstop.

What if the SBA rejects my OIC?

Submit revised offer or file bankruptcy. Rejection strengthens your bankruptcy case by showing good-faith resolution attempt.

Check your bankruptcy discharge eligibility with our free screening tool.
Free Discharge Screener

About This Data: Content based on federal bankruptcy law (Title 11, U.S. Code) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. This is educational content, not legal advice.

## SBA Collections Timeline - What to Expect After Default
Source: https://sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com/sba-collections-timeline.html

SBA Collections Timeline

What to Expect After Default

Home » SBA Collections Timeline

Lender Collection (0-6 months)

The private lender initiates collection: demand letters, acceleration, possibly a lawsuit. Negotiate directly - workouts avoid triggering the SBA guarantee.

SBA Guarantee Purchase (3-12 months)

The lender submits a claim. SBA pays the lender and becomes your creditor. You now have two potential creditors.

SBA and Treasury Collection (6-24 months)

Demand letters, OIC option, then Treasury Offset and cross-servicing. Administrative fees up to 28% added.

DOJ Litigation (18+ months)

Full lawsuit for amount plus interest and penalties. Filing bankruptcy at any point stops everything.

Frequently Asked Questions

When is the best time to file?

Earlier is better. Before Treasury referral avoids the 28% fee surcharge. Before judgment prevents liens.

Can I negotiate after Treasury referral?

Yes, but the balance is now higher. A bankruptcy attorney strengthens your position.

How long can the SBA pursue me?

10-year litigation SOL, but Treasury offset has no limit. Bankruptcy is the only permanent stop.

Check your bankruptcy discharge eligibility with our free screening tool.
Free Discharge Screener

About This Data: Content based on federal bankruptcy law (Title 11, U.S. Code) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. This is educational content, not legal advice.

## SBA Microloan Default - Options and Bankruptcy
Source: https://sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com/sba-microloan-default.html

SBA Microloan Default

Options and Bankruptcy

Home » SBA Microloan Default

Microloan Overview

Loans up to $50,000 through nonprofit intermediaries. May not require personal guarantees. Less collateral than larger SBA loans.

Default Consequences

The intermediary attempts collection, reports to credit bureaus, and may pursue legal action. Collection is often less aggressive due to smaller amounts.

Bankruptcy Treatment

Fully dischargeable. In Chapter 7, eliminated completely. In Chapter 13, treated as general unsecured. A microloan alone may not justify bankruptcy.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do microloans require a personal guarantee?

Depends on the intermediary. Check your documents. If no guarantee, only business assets are at risk.

Can I just ignore a default?

Not advisable. Even small debts can result in judgments and garnishment.

Is a microloan small enough to handle without bankruptcy?

Under $10,000, negotiation is often practical. For larger amounts or combined with other debts, bankruptcy addresses everything at once.

Check your bankruptcy discharge eligibility with our free screening tool.
Free Discharge Screener

About This Data: Content based on federal bankruptcy law (Title 11, U.S. Code) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. This is educational content, not legal advice.

## Subchapter V for SBA Borrowers - Small Business Reorganization
Source: https://sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com/subchapter-v-sba.html

Subchapter V for SBA Borrowers

Small Business Reorganization

Home » Subchapter V for SBA Borrowers

Why Subchapter V Is Ideal

Debtor stays in control, no creditors' committee, no disclosure statement, and the plan can be confirmed over SBA objections. Restructure debt and keep the business alive.

Plan Requirements

Pay projected disposable income for 3-5 years. Not discriminate unfairly. Be fair and equitable. Be feasible. The plan can modify SBA loan terms.

SBA Loan Modification

Extend maturity, reduce interest rate to market rate, strip lien to collateral value, or surrender collateral and discharge the deficiency.

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does Subchapter V cost?

Attorney fees $15,000-$30,000. Filing fee $1,738. The trustee is paid from plan payments.

Can sole proprietors use Subchapter V?

Yes. The plan addresses both business and personal debts in one case.

What if the plan fails?

Convert to Chapter 7 or dismiss. Having a realistic plan is critical.

Check your bankruptcy discharge eligibility with our free screening tool.
Free Discharge Screener

About This Data: Content based on federal bankruptcy law (Title 11, U.S. Code) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. This is educational content, not legal advice.

## Privacy Policy - sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com
Source: https://sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com/privacy.html

sbaborrowerbankruptcy.com

Home
Privacy

Privacy Policy

How we handle your data. Short version: we don't sell it, share it, or use it for advertising.

Data We Collect

We use Google Analytics (GA4) to measure aggregate site traffic. This includes page views, session duration, approximate geographic region (country and city level), and device type. We do not collect names, email addresses, or personally identifiable information through analytics.

Aggregate event data from Google Analytics is also exported to Google BigQuery for research use. The BigQuery data is used only for internal trajectory analysis and network health reports, is not shared with third parties, and is not used for advertising.

What We Don't Do

We do not sell data. We do not run advertising. We do not collect financial information. We do not use retargeting. We do not share any user data with attorneys, law firms, creditors, debt collectors, or legal service providers.

Specifically, we do not enable Google Signals, Google Ads integration, User-ID collection, or any cross-device tracking feature in Google Analytics. GA4 operates in aggregate-traffic-measurement mode only on this site.

Cookies

We use only first-party cookies set by Google Analytics for aggregate traffic measurement. We do not use advertising cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party analytics cookies.

Third-Party Services

Google Analytics (GA4) - aggregate traffic measurement

Google BigQuery - aggregate event data warehouse for research use only; not shared, not used for advertising

Google Search Console - search query measurement (clicks, impressions, our own page rankings)

GitHub Pages - hosting

Contact Forms & Email

This site may link to contact forms or email addresses operated by the Open Bankruptcy Project. Any data you submit through those channels is handled under the Open Bankruptcy Project master privacy policy, which governs all sites in this network.

Children's Privacy

This site is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect information from children.

Data Retention

Analytics data is retained for 14 months per Google's standard retention settings. After that window, individual event data is automatically deleted by Google; aggregate report metrics remain available.

Your Rights

You may request deletion of any personal data we hold by contacting info@openbankruptcyproject.org. California residents have additional rights under CCPA. EU residents have additional rights under GDPR. All such requests are handled by the Open Bankruptcy Project.

Network Policy

This site is part of the Bankruptcy Transparency Network operated by the Open Bankruptcy Project, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. The same privacy standards apply across every site in the network. The authoritative master policy is maintained at https://openbankruptcyproject.org/privacy.html.

Changes

We may update this policy from time to time. Changes are reflected on the master policy first and then mirrored across all network sites. Check the master policy for the most current version.

Effective: March 30, 2026  |  Updated: April 11, 2026

Home  ·  Privacy  ·  Open Bankruptcy Project

Part of the Bankruptcy Transparency Network. Educational information only - not legal advice.

No ads. No affiliate links. No data sold. Supported by donations.
